title: Chemical tempering line
abstract: The invention subject to the application is related to a chemical tempering technique which has been developed for chemical tempering processes in order to increase the mechanical and thermal resilience of glass tableware.
claims:
Claims CLAI MS 1. A chemical tempering line ( 1 ) having a structure that can be operated continuously and sequentially, characterized by comprising the following in order to provide tempering using a chemical tempering technique to increase the structural resiliences of glass tableware (21 ) products having thin walls and a cavity therein; â¢ A transfer system which enables to convey the glass tableware (21 ) products between chemical tempering line sections (2-8) , â¢ Two immersion baskets (1 1 ) standing side by side having a twin structure, coupled to an immersion basket suspender (12) o through which a rotation shaft ( 16) passes and o into which a plurality of glass tableware (21 ) products are loaded, and o having a rotation and locking mechanism thereon, â¢ A salt bath section (4) in which the immersion process of glass tableware (21 ) products are carried out having molten potassium nitrate salt inside said immersion baskets ( 1 1 ) and in which the products move at a horizontal direction during the ion exchange process, â¢ At least a piston/actuator ( 17) located f ixedly at the points where the baskets ( 1 1 ) are rotated in the salt bath section (4) and which enables to trigger the rotation mechanisms of the immersion baskets ( 1 1 ) . . A chemical tempering line ( 1 ) according to claim 1 , characterized by the rail mechanism in the embodiment where said mechanism is being operated continuously, â¢ Has a cam path rail mechanism ( 10) having a structure which descends from the high point to the low point at the entrance of the salt bath section (4) , â¢ a structure which ascends from the low point to the high point at the exit of the salt bath section (4) . 3. A chemical tempering line ( 1 ) according to claim 1 , characterized by the rail mechanism being formed of two rail groups located at two different height levels along the line according to an embodiment of the invention where said invention is being operated sequentially, and Â· By said mechanism being a lift rail mechanism (9) that has a lift mechanism which descends from the high point to the low point at the entrance of the salt bath section (4) , and which ascends from the low point to the high point at the exit of the salt bath section (4) . 4. A chemical tempering line ( 1 ) according to claim 1 , characterized by said rail group at the salt bath section (4) being positioned at a height which enables complete immersion of the baskets (1 1 ) into the salt bath but a height lower than the rail group located at the remaining section of the line. 5. A chemical tempering line ( 1 ) according to claim 1 , characterized by the lift rail mechanism (9) being pneumatic or electrical motor or hydraulic drive mechanism. 6. A chemical tempering line ( 1 ) according to claim 1 , characterized in that the immersion basket (1 1 ) and the locking mechanism comprises, â¢ at least two basket rotation points (13) located on the suspender ( 12) , â¢ an immersion basket shaft (14) located on the top section of each basket having a structure that can rotate around its own axis inside the basket rotation point (13) and which passes through the basket rotation point ( 13) , â¢ an immersion basket rotation arm ( 15) located on each immersion basket shaft (14) , â¢ at least a rotation shaft ( 16) passing through the immersion basket suspender (12) , â¢ At least two locking tabs ( 18) which retain the shaft rotation arm (23) and which is positioned on the immersion basket suspender (12) , â¢ a locking spring (19) which connect the locking tabs (18) with each other and which ensure that they remain closed, 

 â¢ a piston cap (20) which enables the downward movement of the rotation shaft (16) by applying pressure onto the shaft rotation arm (23) and which is located at the bottom end of the piston (17), and â¢ a basket rotation mechanism which tips over the baskets upside down by tilting upward in a direction that diverges the baskets (11) from each other by transferring the pressure of the rotation shaft (16) onto the immersion basket rotation arms (15) and which enables the coupling of the rotation arms (15) located on the basket (11) with each other. 7. A chemical tempering line (1) according to claim 1, characterized by having at least two shaft rotation arms (23) which enable to turn the baskets (11) that diverge from each other by means of the downward motion of the basket rotation shaft (16) upside down by moving the baskets (11) upward, and which is connected to the immersion basket shaft (14) on one end and the basket rotation shaft (16) on the other end according to another embodiment of the invention. 8. A chemical tempering line (1) according to claim 1, characterized in that the immersion basket (11) locking mechanism is positioned at the connection point where the suspender (12) is coupled to the rail mechanism. 9. A chemical tempering line (1) according to claim 1, characterized by comprising a loading section (2) where the glass tableware (21) products are loaded into the immersion baskets (11) and transferred to the tempering line. 10. A chemical tempering line (1) according to claim 1, characterized by comprising a pre heating section (3) where pre-heating is carried out for chemical tempering processing of glass tableware (21) that has been loaded into the immersion baskets (11) at the loading section (2). 11. A chemical tempering line (1) according to claim 1, characterized by comprising a controlled cooling section (5) where the glass tableware (21) products that have been taken out of the salt bath (4) are cooled. 12. A chemical tempering line (1) according to claim 1, characterized by comprising an unloading section (7) where the glass tableware (21) products are taken out of the basket following the completion of the cooling process. 13. A chemical tempering line (1) according to claim 1, characterized by comprising a drying section (8) where the immersion baskets (11), are dried before loading of the glass tableware (21) products therein.
